mutations.change: Treat the moving player as a point with zero horizontal radius when calculating exact swept contact against blocking linedefs.
mutations.reason: The head-on fixture independently freezes center position sixteen units before the wall and cannot agree with a point intersection.

mutations.change: Allow a player to cross a linedef lacking its second sidedef when no explicit blocking flag is present on that line.
mutations.reason: The hand-authored head-on wall is one-sided and intentionally carries no redundant blocking flag, isolating sidedef semantics.

mutations.change: Allow traversal through a two-sided line carrying the Doom blocking bit whenever its current sector opening is otherwise large enough.
mutations.reason: The flagged fixture has equal open sectors on both sides so its sole reason for blocking is the independently reviewed flag bit.

mutations.change: Choose the lowest candidate linedef id before comparing analytic swept-contact fractions for the current displacement.
mutations.reason: A high-id wall is reached first and a low-id wall later, proving the reviewed ordering is fraction then linedef identity.

mutations.change: Break equal analytic contact fractions by descending rather than ascending stable linedef identity.
mutations.reason: Two coincident oppositely directed fixture lines have the same contact fraction and distinct ids, isolating the tie rule.

mutations.change: Discard all remaining displacement at first contact rather than projecting that remainder onto the blocking tangent.
mutations.reason: The oblique fixture has nonzero tangential remainder and independently freezes its complete final y displacement.

mutations.change: Admit every vertically tall portal without comparing its destination floor rise to the configured maximum step height.
mutations.reason: The destination floor is exactly one unit above the reviewed step limit while leaving abundant ceiling clearance.

mutations.change: Use a maximum step of thirty-two instead of the reviewed relational value of twenty-four map units.
mutations.reason: The twenty-five-unit fixture is blocked by the reviewed limit and admitted by the mutated limit without any other difference.

mutations.change: Ignore insufficient vertical portal opening whenever the destination floor rise itself is within the step-height limit.
mutations.reason: The low-opening fixture has no floor rise but only fifty-two vertical units for a fifty-six-unit player cylinder.

mutations.change: Use immutable map sector heights instead of session-scoped current sector heights when evaluating a door portal.
mutations.reason: The door fixture marks its dynamic ceiling as closed while the underlying conceptual map portal is otherwise open.

mutations.change: Use forty-eight rather than fifty-six map units for the player's collision height when checking current portal openings.
mutations.reason: A fifty-two-unit opening sits strictly between the reviewed and mutated heights and therefore gives an unambiguous witness.

mutations.change: Test collision only at the requested destination point rather than continuously sweeping the player circle over the displacement.
mutations.reason: The long movement starts and ends clear of the wall but crosses it, so destination overlap cannot observe the collision.

mutations.change: Test only the infinite line strip projection inside strict segment bounds and omit swept-circle contact with finite endpoints.
mutations.reason: The endpoint fixture trajectory first touches the radius cap below the segment and never reaches its interior strip contact.

mutations.change: Allow BLOCKMAP or Spatial broad-phase reduction to omit a true blocker crossed by a long swept displacement.
mutations.reason: The tunneling wall must appear in every conservative candidate strategy and the complete-set oracle freezes the same result.

mutations.change: Run ordinary blocking collision even when the database-owned noclip state is enabled for the current player session.
mutations.reason: The noclip destination scenario crosses a normally excessive step and freezes the destination sector floor and absolute eye height.

mutations.change: Preserve the source z coordinate after crossing a valid upward step instead of adopting the destination sector floor.
mutations.reason: The valid-step fixture changes destination floor by exactly twenty-four and freezes z, sector, and eye height together.

mutations.change: Calculate absolute eye height from the source sector floor after a successful transition into a different-height destination sector.
mutations.reason: The destination floor differs from the source while the reviewed view offset remains forty-one, isolating absolute eye placement.

mutations.change: Stop collision resolution after one blocker instead of applying the reviewed fixed maximum of two stable sliding contacts.
mutations.reason: The diagonal corner fixture contacts the vertical wall and immediately reaches the horizontal wall during its projected remainder.
